business.headline: Gold at Rs 1.52 lakh, silver near Rs 2.47 lakh: Should you stay invested or sell?Gold and silver prices are moving sharply, and many investors are wondering what to do next. With gold around Rs 1.52 lakh and silver near Rs 2.47 lakh, is it time to stay invested or consider selling?
Govt allows ethanol blending in Aviation Turbine FuelThe move is aimed at promoting Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and reducing India's dependence on imported oil.
Why more roads won't fix Gurugram's traffic crisisGurugram's population has doubled from 1.5 million in 2011 to over 3 million today. Only 12 to 15% of residents regularly use public transport.
Sensex ends 757 points lower, Nifty below 24,400; HCLTech sinks 11%The S&P BSE Sensex tanked 756.84 points to close at 78,516.49, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 198.50 points to end at 24,378.10.

Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights to save fuel costs as prices surgeJet fuel is one of the largest cost components for airlines, and a surge in prices has made several short-haul routes unviable. To manage this, Lufthansa is pulling back from unprofitable routes.

Do you need to file ITR if you over 80? Here's what you should knowTurning 80 comes with a few tax relaxations, but does it mean you can skip filing your ITR altogether? The rules are not that simple. Here's what you should know before assuming you are exempt.

Petrol, diesel prices unchanged in India, but for how long?Global crude is rising amid the West Asia war, but India's petrol and diesel prices have not changed since May 2022. How much longer can this continue?

Selling your old car? Here's how to transfer no claim bonus before it is goneOne important thing to understand is that your No Claim Bonus belongs to you and not your car. When you sell your vehicle, the NCB does not get transferred to the new owner.

Can a car lease still help you save tax under the new regime?The new tax regime is often seen as simpler but with fewer perks. Yet, some benefits continue quietly in the background. One of them is car leasing. Could this be an easy way to save tax? Let's have a look.

8th Pay Commission meetings begin soon: What should you expect?With the 8th Pay Commission meetings set to begin soon, government employees are watching closely for updates on salaries and pensions. Will these discussions bring any big changes to your pay package?

RBI changes auto-debit rules: Your Netflix, SIPs and bill payments may get easierThe RBI has raised the no-OTP auto-debit limit to Rs 15,000, making recurring payments like subscriptions, SIPs, insurance premiums and bills smoother, faster and less prone to failure.

NSE nears IPO after Sebi panel clears Rs 1,800 crore settlementThe Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) expert panel has approved NSE's proposal to settle long-pending cases by paying around Rs 1,800 crore.

Sensex tumbles 800 points: 4 things investors must knowAt around 9:50 am, the BSE Sensex was down 628.98 points, or 0.79%, at 78,644.35, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 162.80 points, or 0.66%, to 24,413.80.

HCLTech, TCS, Infosys tumble up to 9%: Why are IT stocks falling today?At around 9:18 am, HCLTech was the biggest hit among IT stocks, plunging 9.1% to Rs 1,310.05 after its earnings update. Infosys fell 2.5% to Rs 1,280.85, Tata Consultancy Services slipped 1.7% to Rs 2,565, while Tech Mahindra dropped 3.8% to Rs 1,443.30.

HCLTech shares crash 10% after Q4 results. Why is the stock falling?The stock fell 9.64% to Rs 1,302.60 at 9:18 am, making it the biggest loser among frontline technology names. Other IT stocks also fell sharply in early trade.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade, dragged by IT stocks; HCLTech falls 10%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 580.11 points to 78,693.22, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 142.60 points to 24,434.00 as of 9:42 am.

Why your favourite Diet Coke is suddenly disappearing from stores this summerSupply bottlenecks, delayed shipments and a summer demand surge are making Diet Coke harder to find on store shelves and quick-commerce apps across several cities.

RBI updates e-mandate rules for transactions over Rs 15,000. Check detailsUnder the new guidelines, recurring payments of up to Rs 15,000 will not require a one-time password (OTP). However, transactions above this threshold will need additional authentication.

India must pursue energy independence; fuel price cushion won't last: Gita GopinathIndia needs to speed up its shift towards energy independence as the current strategy of holding fuel prices steady becomes increasingly difficult to sustain, former IMF chief economist Gita Gopinath said, warning that the economy's present resilience could come under pressure if the Iran-US war prolongs.

Gita Gopinath decodes Iran war's impact on India and global economyIn an exclusive interview with India Today Consulting Editor Rajdeep Sardesai, Harvard University professor and former IMF Deputy Managing Director Gita Gopinath discussed the economic implications of the ongoing Iran conflict.

entertainment.headline: Michael first reviews: Jaafar Jackson shines as King of Pop but biopic lacks depth The first reviews of the musical biopic Michael have praised Jaafar Jackson as the King of Pop, Michael Jackson. The film is set for a theatrical release on April 24.

Raja Shivaji: Riteish Deshmukh defends Wagh Nakh scene, says watch the film Actor Riteish Deshmukh responded to criticism over a scene in Raja Shivaji's trailer after users questioned its historical accuracy. He urged audiences to watch the film on May 1 before forming an opinion.

Abhishek Bachchan calls Aishwarya Rai his soulmate, recalls love story Days after their 19th wedding anniversary, Abhishek Bachchan opened up about his love story with Aishwarya Rai Bachchan. He called her his soulmate and recalled how the couple fell in love.

Dhurandhar 2 smashes Pathaan to become UK's No. 1 Indian film in 34 days Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues its record-breaking run, overtaking Pathaan to become the highest-grossing Indian film in the UK. With strong numbers overseas and a dominant global performance, the film's box office journey seems to be far from slowing down.

Hansika Motwani's casting couch remark draws flak: Another one living in a bubble Hansika Motwani's recent remarks on the casting couch have drawn sharp criticism, with many calling her comments dismissive of the issue.

Shashi Tharoor turns photographer for actor Priyanka Mohan at Rashtrapati Bhavan Actor Priyanka Mohan attended the prestigious President's banquet at Rashtrapati Bhavan hosted for South Korean President Lee Jae-myung. The event celebrated cultural exchange and diplomatic relations between India and South Korea with notable guests from politics and entertainment.

Actor Ajith returns to Chennai from Belgium to vote in Tamil Nadu elections. Watch Renowned actor and racer Ajith Kumar has returned to Chennai to cast his vote in the Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections on April 23. Fans greeted him enthusiastically as he arrived from Belgium.

Salman Khan begins director Vamshi's film with his signature swag, Nayanthara joins Salman Khan and Vamshi Paidipally's film was launched with a pooja ceremony in Mumbai. The makers later released a video of both Nayanthara and Salman attending the event.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do: Ayushmann, Sara, Wamiqa and Rakul in peppy Punjabi song The first song from Pati Patni Aur Woh Do, titled Roop Di Rani has been released. The high-energy Punjabi song features Ayushmann Khurrana, Sara Ali Khan, Wamiqa Gabbi and Rakul Preet Singh.

Actor Siddhu Jonnalagadda to star in Venky Atluri's web series: Report Actor Siddhu Jonnalagadda, who has several projects in the pipeline, is reportedly set to return to the OTT space after a gap. More details about the project are still awaited.

Is Allu Arjun planning to shift base to Mumbai? His father breaks silence Rumours were rife that Allu Arjun is planning to shift to Mumbai to work on his upcoming projects. His father and producer Allu Aravind broke his silence on the speculation.

Anubhav Sinha explains why Assi underperformed at the box office Anubhav Sinha said his film Assi underperformed at the box office despite strong critical praise and audience appreciation. He pointed to changing viewing habits and the diverse tastes of Indian audiences as key reasons for its theatrical performance.

Vidyut Jammwal's Gayatri Mantra rendition at CinemaCon 2026 stuns audience. Video Vidyut Jammwal created a powerful moment at CinemaCon 2026, blending spirituality with his big Hollywood reveal. His unexpected rendition of Gayatri Mantra sparked global conversation.

Strict rules and rising caution: How NESCO drug case reshapes Mumbai's live events Mumbai's live events industry is bracing for unprecedented scrutiny following the NESCO incident, where two youngsters allegedly died of a drug overdose. It is set to redefine safety protocols and regulatory oversight across the city's entertainment landscape in the coming months.

Akshay Kumar reacts to criticism over Samrat Prithviraj's fake moustache Akshay Kumar addressed the criticism over his fake moustache in Samrat Prithviraj. He said that the look was required for the role and couldn't be achieved naturally.

Ranveer Singh moves to end Don 3 row with Farhan Akhtar, may return Rs 10 crore fee The Don 3 controversy takes a new turn as reports claim Ranveer Singh may have taken a major step to resolve his fallout with Farhan Akhtar.

Vijay Varma steps out with Jawan actor Aaliyah Qureishi, viral video sparks buzz Vijay Varma and Aaliyah Qureishi's Mumbai outing goes viral after the duo were seen leaving a restaurant and chatting inside a car.

Ranbir Kapoor nailed Lord Rama's look in Ramayana, says director Anurag Basu Director Anurag Basu, who has collaborated with Ranbir Kapoor on Jagga Jasoos and Barfi!, praised his look in Ramayana while highlighting the challenge of the role. Ramayana will release in theatres on Diwali this year.

Panchayat actor Vinod Suryavanshi recalls caste abuse, colour bias in raw account Vinod Suryavanshi, known for Panchayat, opened up about growing up amid caste discrimination in his village. From rejection over his looks to deeply personal struggles, his journey to the screen is far more layered than it appears.

general news.headline: India-bound ship among two vessels attacked, seized by Iran in HormuzThe incident is bound to escalate tensions between Iran and India after two India-flagged ships were attacked last week in the Strait of Hormuz.

Mallikarjun Kharge gets EC's notice over 'terrorist' remark against PMBJP accused the Congress of violating the model code of conduct and complained against Kharge to the poll body. Following that, the Election Commission took a quick action.
Ex-servant accused of strangling IRS officer's daughter at Delhi home caughtEx-servant accused of strangling IRS officer's daughter with charger cable at Delhi home caught

A conspiracy to get Pakistan water from India under Indus treaty?As summer temperatures rise, the pressure on Pakistan is mounting due to India keeping the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance after the Pahalgam terror attack. But an insidious game is on to make India relent even as the West plays along Pakistan's latest peacemaker farce.

Terror angle probed in India refinery fire; Iraq oil facility latest in global patternA refinery in Iraq's Ebril was hit by an explosion on Wednesday, joining a list of energy installations across five countries in the world that have seen fires and blasts. A team of NIA, India's counter-terror agency, started a probe at HPCL's Rajasthan refinery, which saw a massive blaze on Monday, just a day ahead of its inauguration.

Get out, you are causing jam: Frustrated woman berates BJP minister at rallyFrustrated by the standstill, a Mumbai woman stepped out of her vehicle, walked into the middle of the march, confronted the BJP minister and got into a heated argument with police officers.

To recuse or not: Two judges, two decisions and the question of conscienceTwo judges, in two separate courts, took two different calls on recusal on the same day while hearing cases involving political leaders Arvind Kejriwal and Rahul Gandhi. These developments have brought into focus a key question: what governs judicial recusal in India?

Even chips being seized: How protests against Nepal PM have Indian border linkWithin a month of taking over as Nepal's Prime Minister, Balen Shah is facing protests. The protests are linked to Nepal's enforcement of customs duties at the Indo-Nepal border, which is restricting the easy flow of goods and making products costlier for Nepalese people. Videos of clashes between the Nepal police and people at the border checkpoints have emerged.

Is ChatGPT fuelling divorces and breakups in India?Across the US and parts of Europe, there is a growing pile of anecdotal evidence—partners turning to AI not just for advice, but for companionship, validation, even intimacy. Is it very much like that in India?

Does Andrew Hugg, the mysterious US N-official caught in a sting, even exist?A lesser-known American media outlet conducted a sting operation on Andrew Hugg, an official it claimed to be at the centre of America's nuclear and chemical safety apparatus. A string of articles on Hugg followed, but his identity couldn't be independently verified. For now, Hugg remains a mystery.

IRS officer's daughter strangled in Delhi, parents were at gym; ex-servant suspectPolice said the woman was alone at home when the crime occurred after her parents stepped out in the morning. Her body was later found under suspicious circumstances, following which the police reached the spot.

Why Election Commission suddenly asked tourists to leave Bengal's beach resortsThe Election Commission, in a rare directive, has directed hotels and resorts in Bengal not to accommodate people who are not from the local constituency. This comes after a ban on plying of bikes and confusion over an unprecedented 96-hour liquor ban.

E85 in India: Are we ready for the next big fuel leap?As India adjusts to E20, the sudden shift in conversation toward E85 raises critical questions. The transition is far more complex and still appears distant. But the real question is: are we ready, and what challenges will E85 bring?

70-80% of politicians watch porn: Another shocker from Pappu YadavIndependent MP Pappu Yadav sparked fresh controversy after claiming that 70–80 per cent of politicians watch pornography. He earlier sparked a row when he claimed that "90 per cent of women cannot enter politics without going into a male leader's room".

Indian tourists caught stealing hotel items in Bali, staff check luggageThe controversy gained traction after an X user, Tarun Gautam, shared a video criticising the Indian tourists and called the episode embarrassing. The clip appears to show Bali hotel staff inspecting luggage during checkout as the Indian tourists waited in the vicinity.

North to sizzle in heatwave, northeast to experience thunderous WednesdayTemperatures across northwest and central India are expected to climb by another 3–5C before finally dipping towards the end of the month. But before that, here's what's in store.

Did crypto scam by fake IRGC trigger real IRGC firing on Indian ship in Hormuz?Did a crypto scam involving fake clearance leave an Indian ship caught off guard when Iranian forces opened fire in the Strait of Hormuz, despite the crew believing they had permission to transit? Find out.

Walked in during ED raid on I-PAC, put democracy in peril: SC raps Mamata BanerjeeThe Supreme Court came down heavily on West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee for entering the premises of an ED raid on the residence of I-PAC co-founder Pratik Jain in January, saying such conduct puts "democracy in peril".

RBI changes auto-debit rules: Your Netflix, SIPs and bill payments may get easierThe RBI has raised the no-OTP auto-debit limit to Rs 15,000, making recurring payments like subscriptions, SIPs, insurance premiums and bills smoother, faster and less prone to failure.

Indian medical student uses AI woman to earn thousands of dollars from 'dumb Americans'A 22-year-old Indian medical student created an AI woman resembling Jennifer Lawrence and Sydney Sweeney and used it to make thousands of dollars from Republican Party supporters, whom he termed "dumb". He even sold nudes of Emily Hart, the AI model, to paid subscribers, reported WIRED.

Hansika Motwani's casting couch remark draws flak: Another one living in a bubbleHansika Motwani's recent remarks on the casting couch have drawn sharp criticism, with many calling her comments dismissive of the issue.

High performers at work are punished: Mumbai man says Gen Z has spotted the 'trap'A man from Mumbai explained why Gen Z employees avoid going "above and beyond," emphasising how high performers often faced burnout instead of rewards.

Trump didn't extend Iran ceasefire. He launched an economic weaponTrump's move to extend the Iran ceasefire is widely being seen as a softening of his stance, especially after earlier threats to resume bombing. However, behind it is a calculated strategy to permanently damage Iran's oil fields - its economic lifeline.

A year after attack, Pahalgam's Baisaran struggles with empty trails, broken livesOne year after the Pahalgam terror attack, the Baisaran meadows remain shut, leaving ponywallahs, hoteliers and local traders mired in a prolonged tourism slump with no clear path to recovery.

sports.headline: Puducherry cricket body suspends 3 players on charges of assault on U-19 state coach The Cricket Association of Pondicherry (CAP) has suspended three of its first-class cricketers with immediate effect on allegations that they assaulted U-19 state team coach Venkataraman on December 8 last year.

LSG vs RR: After 353-day injury layoff, pacer Mayank Yadav makes IPL return Lucknow Super Giants received a timely boost as tearaway pacer Mayank Yadav returned to the playing XI after a 353-day injury layoff, with captain Rishabh Pant opting to bowl first against Rajasthan Royals at the Ekana Stadium on Tuesday.

MI vs CSK: MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma face late fitness tests ahead of El Clasico IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma are set to undergo late fitness tests ahead of the much-anticipated Mumbai Indians vs Chennai Super Kings clash.

LSG vs RR Live: Vaibhav fails at Ekana, Shami, Mohsin leave Rajasthan reeling Lucknow (LSG) vs Rajasthan (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026: RR (44/3 in 6 ov). Mohammed Shami starts well for Lucknow Super Giants. He dismisses Yashasvi Jaiswal and Dhruv Jurel on back-to-back deliveries to leave Rajasthan reeling at the Ekana. After Shami's brilliance, Mohsin Khan doubles down with a maiden-wicket over of Sooryavanshi.

Real Madrid star Bellingham invests in cricket: Buys stake in Hundred franchise Jude Bellingham steps into cricket with a stake in Birmingham Phoenix. The Real Madrid star's investment reflects both his sporting interests and deep-rooted connection to Birmingham.

PAK star under investigation after testing positive for recreational drug use T20 World Cup 2026: Pakistan's Mohammad Nawaz faces a PCB probe after a reported positive drug test during the T20 World Cup, with his Surrey deal cancelled and his immediate cricketing future now uncertain.

Probably learnt nothing: Andy Murray reflects on coaching Novak Djokovic Andy Murray reflects on his coaching stint with Novak Djokovic, calling it insightful despite no major wins, while backing the Serbian to bounce back from injury and contend for another Grand Slam.

Rs 50 crore wasted? Pant, Pooran under pressure to justify price-tag IPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants face mounting pressure as their most expensive duo, Rishabh Pant and Nicholas Pooran struggle for form. With a crucial clash against Rajasthan Royals next, the spotlight is firmly on experienced duo to deliver.

IPL 2026: If Pat Cummins returns, should SRH stick with Ishan Kishan as captain? IPL 2026: Pat Cummins' return leaves SRH with a big captaincy call, as Ishan Kishan's strong stint sparks debate over whether the franchise should stick with continuity or revert to experience.

IPL teams not getting it right vs left-handers? Orange Cap standings tell a story Indian Premier League 2026 numbers highlight a clear trend in batting dominance. Left-handers are thriving at the top, while teams continue to struggle with tactics and trust issues in handling matchups against them.

Venus Williams targets return to French Open after five years despite lean patch Venus Williams eyes a French Open return despite a 10-match losing streak, as the veteran continues her clay comeback after a first-round exit at the Madrid Open.

What was Axar Patel thinking? DC captain slammed for tactical blunders vs SRHIPL 2026: Axar Patel faces heavy criticism after Delhi Capitals' tactical missteps vs SRH. Overusing a part-timer while underutilising key spinners proved costly as DC lost control in a high-scoring clash on Tuesday.

Was Kagiso Rabada smoking in Gujarat Titans team hotel? Viral video sparks buzzA video clip has gone viral on social media which allegedly shows Gujarat Titans' Kagiso Rabada smoking what looked to be a cigarette.

Premier League glory in 2016, Leicester City relegated to England's 3rd divisionLeicester City have been relegated to League One after a draw against Hull City. The 2016 Premier League champions complete a stunning fall, dropping to England's third tier after back-to-back relegations and a troubled campaign.

Next demi-god of cricket? BCCI urged to act fast on Vaibhav SooryavanshiKris Srikkanth urged the BCCI to fast-track 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the Indian senior team, suggesting that he could be the next legendary figure in the sport after Sachin Tendulkar.

SRH vs DC: Delhi Capitals reflect on Nitish Rana ploy that ultimately backfiredDelhi Capitals' Venugopal Rao addresses the controversial decision to bowl Nitish Rana for his full quota and the tactical middle-order collapse that handed SRH a 47-run victory in Hyderabad.

World body revokes India Volleyball Federation recognition after players' protestFIVB has revoked recognition of Volleyball Federation of India after governance breaches. The move comes days after player protests exposed deeper issues around facilities, selection politics and athlete welfare in Indian volleyball.

FIFA adds more World Cup 2026 tickets after backlash over pricey new categoriesFIFA faces backlash after hiking FIFA World Cup 2026 ticket prices and adding premium categories. The fresh ticket release aims to ease concerns, but questions over transparency and affordability remain unresolved.

What makes Sakib Hussain stand out? Aaron hails SRH pacer after crucial DC spellIPL 2026: Varun Aaron pinpoints the key trait behind Sakib Hussain's rise after SRH's win over DC. His pace variation and hunger are turning Hyderabad's bowling from a weakness into a genuine strength.

LSG vs RR: Sooryavanshi in town as Lucknow hope to escape their backyard trapIPL 2026, RR vs LSG preview: Lucknow Super Giants are trapped in their own labyrinth. With their big guns misfiring and the season sliding away, the task only gets tougher. Now, a determined Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the Rajasthan Royals have arrived in town, looking to find their own path back to winning ways on Wednesday.

Abhishek Sharma matches Virat Kohli, eclipses Chris Gayle with blazing 135: In StatsIPL 2026, SRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma was in outstanding form on Tuesday, scoring an unbeaten 135 off 68 balls for Sunrisers Hyderabad against Delhi Capitals, guiding the Orange Army to a 47-run victory.

Play of the Day: Abhishek Sharma bluffs DC into bowling 4 overs off Nitish RanaSRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma punished Delhi Capitals for the series of errors that they committed at the Uppal on Tuesday. Some smart batting from the young left-hander forced Delhi into committing a cardinal sin: bowling their part-timer for 4 full overs.

Rahul's batting intent, wicketkeeping skills questioned after DC stumble vs SRHAakash Chopra criticised KL Rahul's batting intent and wicketkeeping after Delhi Capitals' heavy defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad in IPL 2026, questioning his approach in the 243-run chase. Rahul struggled with the bat and behind the stumps in the loss.

An off-day: Axar Patel downplays Delhi Capitals' heavy loss to Sunrisers HyderabadIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel termed the 47-run loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad as an "off day," while admitting that fielding lapses cost his side despite Abhishek Sharma's explosive unbeaten 135 powering SRH to 242.

Put a camera on him: Abhishek Sharma reveals father's match ritual after 135 vs DCIPL 2026, SRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma revealed a touching father-son ritual after his unbeaten 135 against Delhi Capitals where he won the Player of the Match award after the Sunrisers Hyderabad won by 47 runs.

Abhishek's Hyderabadi special gives DC bitter aftertaste of tactical errorsAbhishek Sharma's unbeaten 135 powered SRH to victory over Delhi Capitals as DC were left regretting poor tactical calls and a slow start in the chase in IPL 2026.

India Women unfazed by Deepti Sharma form slump ahead of T20 World CupSAW vs INDW, 3rd T20I: India Women remain confident in Deepti Sharma despite her prolonged slump, with coach Aavishkar Salvi backing her to bounce back soon. Deepti has scored only two runs and gone wicketless in the series against South Africa.

Abhishek unveils new celebration after hundred vs DC, reveals story behind itAbhishek Sharma revealed the reason behind his new celebration after scoring a stunning unbeaten 135 for Sunrisers Hyderabad against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026.

An un-Abhishek hundred: Opener ditches all-out style, hits 2nd IPL hundredSRH vs DC IPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad opener Abhishek Sharma scored a terrific hundred in his team's home game against Delhi Capitals. Batting first, a patient Abhishek brought up his 2nd IPL century in 47 balls.

Narwal bags gold at Junior World Cup; India takes three medals on opening dayShiva Narwal won gold in the men's 10m air pistol as India secured three medals on the opening day of the ISSF Junior World Cup in Cairo. Panaah Bhugra claimed silver in the women's 10m air rifle, while Chirag Sharma added bronze to cap a strong start for India.

Dhoni sparks CSK return hopes before MI tie, does wicketkeeping drills in netsMS Dhoni has sparked hope for Chennai Super Kings fans ahead of the MI clash after returning to wicketkeeping drills in the nets at Wankhede during IPL 2026. There is no clrity from Chennai about Dhoni's return to the team.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
